title: Shijing: Sheng Min (Birth of Hou Ji)
description: From the Shijing (Book of Songs) — the oldest collection of Chinese poetry, compiled by Confucius from songs of the Zhou dynasty.
introduction: From the Shijing (Book of Songs) — the oldest collection of Chinese poetry, compiled by Confucius from songs of the Zhou dynasty.

translation.text: The first birth of our people was from Jiang Yuan. How did she give birth? She had presented a sacrifice to the Lord on High to take away her childlessness. Then she trod on a toe-print made by God, and was moved. She was thereupon great with child. She nursed and nourished him; and he was Hou Ji, the founder of the Zhou.
